data.title: Zcash Founder Says Mythos Audit Found No Additional Critical Flaws in Zcash Protocol
data.description:
Zcash founder Zooko Wilcox said a security audit of Zcash conducted using Mythos did not identify additional critical vulnerabilities in the Zcash protocol.

According to ChainCatcher, Wilcox said the audit was carried out at the request of Shielded Labs, with Anthropic providing assistance aimed at protecting Zcash users.

Wilcox added that Shielded Labs and other teams are continuing work to strengthen security, and that further updates will be released.

data.title: Pudgy Penguins Halts Pudgy Party Development to Focus on Browser Game Pudgy World
data.description:
Pudgy Penguins has announced it will stop developing its Web3 game Pudgy Party and will shift its focus to the free, browser-based game Pudgy World. According to PANews, the project will concentrate resources on Pudgy World going forward.

In August last year, the NFT brand launched Pudgy Party, a Web3 mobile game developed in partnership with Mythical Games. The game featured fast-paced mini-games, customization, and digital collectibles.

In March this year, Pudgy Penguins launched Pudgy World as a free game that runs in a web browser.

data.title: GEOPOLITICS | Trump Says US Killed Tren De Aragua Leader Niño Guerrero in Strike
data.description: The US killed Tren De Aragua drug cartel leader Niño Guerrero in a strike coordinated with Venezuela, US President Donald Trump said. According to Bloomberg, Trump described the operation as coordinated with Venezuela.

data.title: Sichuan Court Sentences Five in Cross-Border Cyber Pornography Case Involving Virtual Currency Laundering
data.description:
A court in Sichuan sentenced five defendants in a cross-border cyber pornography case that involved laundering funds through virtual currency.

According to NS3.AI, the overseas website linked to the case recorded capital flows of more than 12 million yuan.

The report said one account received more than 8.6 million yuan in illicit funds.

data.title: Ethereum Whale Completes Eight Short-Term Trades Over Two and a Half Days, Mostly Profitable
data.description:
An Ethereum (ETH) swing-trading whale executed eight ETH-only trades over the past two and a half days, taking both long and short positions with holding periods of about one to two hours each. According to BlockBeats On-chain Detection, the trader recorded profits on seven trades and a loss on one, for a win rate of 87%.

The address reportedly grew its trading capital from $3 million to $6.67 million, generating cumulative profits of $3.67 million, representing an approximate return of 122%.

data.title: US XRP Spot ETFs See $2.04 Million Net Inflow on June 12, Led by Canary Fund
data.description:
US XRP spot exchange-traded funds (ETFs) recorded a combined daily net inflow of $2.0443 million yesterday (June 12, Eastern Time), according to SoSoValue data.

According to NS3.AI, the Canary XRP ETF was the only XRP spot ETF to post net inflows during the session.

data.title: Brazilian House Committee Approves Bill Allowing Crypto Freezes in Cyber Fraud Investigations
data.description:
Brazil’s Chamber of Deputies Finance and Taxation Committee approved a bill that would allow authorities to freeze cryptocurrency balances of targets under police investigation for online fraud.

According to ChainCatcher, the proposal also seeks to amend provisions of the Penal Code to increase penalties for cybercrime.

Crimes carried out via social media or phone would face harsher punishment under the bill. It would raise prison sentences for those convicted of cyberattacks to 6 to 10 years, from 4 to 8 years.

The measure would also give judges authority to freeze suspects’ assets held at Bitcoin exchanges and in bank accounts. Additional preventive steps cited include freezing physical assets and banning the use of payment systems.

The bill will next be reviewed by the Constitution and Justice Committee, with consideration expected to be completed in the coming days.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Rejects Recall Calls Over Alleged Jailbreak Vulnerability
data.description:
Anthropic said it disagreed that the existence of a potential jailbreak vulnerability should be grounds to recall commercial AI models that have already been deployed to hundreds of millions of users.

According to Jin10, the company argued that a claimed or possible jailbreak issue, by itself, does not justify pulling back models that are already in commercial use.

Anthropic did not provide additional details in the statement, including the specific model involved, the nature of the alleged vulnerability, or any mitigation measures.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Says U.S. Government Knows of a Method to Bypass Fable 5
data.description:
Anthropic said it understood that the U.S. government believes it has learned of a method to bypass, or “jailbreak,” Fable 5.

According to Jin10, Anthropic said it suspected that no model provider can currently achieve perfect defenses against jailbreak attempts.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Says It Received a U.S. Government Directive Letter
data.description:
Anthropic said it received a directive from the U.S. government at 5:21 p.m. today.

According to Jin10, the company said the letter did not specify details about the government’s national security concerns.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Anthropic Says No Tester Has Found a Universal Jailbreak Method
data.description:
Anthropic said no tester has yet been able to find a universal method to jailbreak its system.

According to Jin10, the company stated that it has not seen testing personnel identify a general-purpose approach that consistently bypasses its safeguards.

data.title: AI TRENDS | U.S. Orders Anthropic to Suspend Access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 for Foreign Nationals
data.description:
The U.S. government issued an export control directive under national security authority requiring a halt to all foreign nationals’ access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5, including Anthropic employees who are foreign nationals. According to Odaily, to comply, Anthropic must shut off all customers’ access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5, while all other Anthropic models are unaffected.

The U.S. government’s oral evidence cited a potential narrow, non-general jailbreak method in the models. Anthropic said it has complied with the legal directive and removed access for all users, but it disagreed with the decision to withdraw deployed commercial models based on the discovery of a narrow potential jailbreak and said it is working to restore access as soon as possible.

data.title: Hyperliquid’s Largest SPCX Long Closes Position at a $300,000 Loss, Analyst Says
data.description:
A wallet described as the largest SPCX long position on Hyperliquid has largely closed its SPCX long, realizing a loss of $300,000, according to on-chain analyst Yu Jin. According to Odaily, the address opened an SPCX long position worth $36.4 million on Hyperliquid before SPCX began trading, with an average entry price of $171.4.

After SPCX opened for trading, its price did not continue rising. The address gradually closed the position over the past several hours at an average price of $169.8, the analyst said.

data.title: U.S. Military Shoots Down Iranian One-Way Attack Drones Headed Toward Strait of Hormuz, Source Says
data.description:
A person familiar with the matter said the U.S. military shot down multiple Iranian one-way attack drones that were flying toward the Strait of Hormuz.

According to Odaily, the information was reported by Reuters, citing the same source.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Multistate Attorneys General Open Investigation Into OpenAI After Broad Subpoena
data.description:
Attorneys general from multiple U.S. states have jointly launched an investigation into OpenAI, according to sources familiar with the matter.

According to NS3.AI, OpenAI received a broad subpoena on Friday seeking documents related to its operations and its impact on users.

OpenAI said it will cooperate with the relevant authorities.

data.title: Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi Says Fees Will Be Charged for Services Related to the Strait of Hormuz
data.description:
Iranian media reported on the 12th that Iranian Foreign Minister Araghchi commented on a draft memorandum of understanding between Iran and the United States, saying fees will be charged for services related to the Strait of Hormuz.

According to Odaily, Araghchi made the remarks the same day while discussing the draft memorandum.

data.title: AI TRENDS | Embodied AI Funding in May 2026 Rose 145% Year Over Year but Fell 58% Month Over Month
data.description: Financing in the embodied AI sector cooled, with funding in May 2026 reaching 8.6 billion yuan, up 145% year over year but down 58% month over month. According to PANews, capital has shifted from complete-machine companies toward core components and data infrastructure, and investment logic has become more rational.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | SPDR Gold Trust Gold Holdings Hold Steady at 1,013.640 Tons
data.description:
SPDR Gold Trust, the world’s largest gold ETF, reported holdings of 1,013.640 tons, unchanged from the previous trading day.

According to Jin10, the fund’s position remained flat compared with the prior session.

data.title: Canada’s Odds to Finish First in World Cup Group B Fall to 27% on Predict.fun
data.description:
Canada’s probability of finishing first in World Cup Group B on predict.fun fell to 27%, down 4 percentage points over the past 24 hours.

According to Odaily, Bosnia and Herzegovina’s probability of finishing first in Group B was 13%.

Earlier this morning, in the first round of Group B group-stage matches, Canada drew 1-1 with Bosnia and Herzegovina.

Canada previously participated in the 1986 and 2022 World Cups, losing all six group-stage matches, scoring two goals and conceding 12.

data.title: Tom Lee Says Funds Are Selling Chip Winners to Raise Cash for a Potential $75 Billion SpaceX IPO
data.description:
Fundstrat Managing Partner Tom Lee said institutional investors are selling recent winners in chip stocks to raise cash ahead of an upcoming SpaceX initial public offering that he valued at $75 billion.

According to NS3.AI, Lee described the rotation out of chip-stock leaders as a healthy consolidation rather than a sign that the broader rally is ending.

Lee said he expects the tech-led uptrend to continue despite the near-term selling tied to IPO-related cash needs.
